title: Authorization, Object Ownership, Properties, Functions, and Tenancy · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs authorization, object ownership, properties, functions, and tenancy from bounded request and server evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the deny-by-default authorization decision matrix. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deny-by-default authorization decision matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.
Load or constrain the target within the authorized tenant and ownership relationship before read, write, delete, export, or nested access.
Allowlist readable and writable properties by operation and principal, reject server-owned fields, and prevent over-posting through explicit command types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Authz Subject Action Resource · changed case · Review Authz Subject Action Resource
steps.instruction:
review case — A review team handles case 7955 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce tenant, action, and property authorization as resource policy; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.

For Go server Authz Subject Action Resource · changed case · Review Authz Subject Action Resource, keep Authz Subject Action Resource connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
